metadata.name: 🏆 Total Drama Franchise Rankings - Complete Cast
improvements: Titles + emojis refreshed to match character archetypes
Kept full reasoning blocks + stats fields for every player

rankings.name: Alejandro
rankings.title: The Snake Charmer
rankings.reasoning: 3 FINALS APPEARANCES (franchise rarity). WINNER of S4 (7-6-1). MOST challenge wins in the franchise (13). Constant heat all game (25 votes against). Top jury pull in the database (12 finalist votes). Strategic mastermind (ranked #1). Saved by Ezekiel's idol play (Ep 8). (S2).
weaknesses: 25 votes (always targeted)
Needed 3 tries to win

rankings.name: Mickey
rankings.title: The Anxious Ace
rankings.reasoning: TWO deep runs with real endgame equity. WINNER of S5 (4-3 (tiebreak)). Strong advantage game (2 idols/advantages found). Found Galang idol. (S6). Played idol on Jay at Final 5. (S6). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 1.50.
weaknesses: Weak challenges (3 wins)
S6: only 1 jury vote

rankings.name: Bowie
rankings.reasoning: S9's dominant winner and the franchise's most advantage-literate player. Bowie played a social spider game from day one, allying with Chase, Damien, Priya, and Millie while quietly hoarding power. He used the Inheritance advantage to strip Julia's idol at Tribal, broke his Unbreakable Bond to blindside Priya, then silenced Julia with a double-vote at F4. He beat Damien at fire to seal a flawless endgame.

rankings.title: The Ice Prince
rankings.reasoning: S6 was a clinic: calm threat management, 4 late immunities, and a 7-1-0 crowning. In S7 he stacked 6 total wins (5 immunities), ran the board with Taylor, then combusted at F4 and quit-turning a near-lock into collapse. With a 50% win rate, 10 challenge wins, and only 2 votes against over two seasons, he's a proven closer when composed. His feud with Carrie and the season's pressure exposed a volatile side that cost him the end.
strengths: Elite challenge streaks to dictate late votes
Builds durable duos (Taylor) to anchor coalitions
weaknesses: Emotional volatility under endgame pressure
High threat profile invites coalition shots
Personal feuds (Carrie) distract strategic focus
rankings.name: Jacques

rankings.title: The Enforcer
rankings.reasoning: MacArthur's S7 was a power-social showcase: she found the Mauhu idol, bridged across blocs, steered key boots, and won the jury with controlled force. She kept her idol holstered correctly through joint tribals and late storms, then locked the endgame with Cody and Carrie. With 4 challenge wins, 1 idol found, and only 5 votes against, her run was efficient and respected. A fiery rivalry with Sky never derailed her path to the crown.
strengths: Cross-tribe relationship building that scales to merge
Excellent idol discipline and timing (pocketing under pressure)
Independent agency on votes that juries reward
weaknesses: Direct style risks conflicts with strong-willed allies
High visibility makes her an immediate returnee target
rankings.name: MacArthur
Cameron
rankings.title: The Brainiac
rankings.reasoning: Reliable challenge contributor (6 total wins). Strong advantage game (2 idols/advantages found). High-end strategist (best rank: #3). Cast tiebreaker for Emma. (S3). Voted Zoey at FTC. (S4). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 4.00.
weaknesses: Only 1 jury vote career
Can't close at FTC

rankings.name: Sanders
rankings.reasoning: S8's most complete non-winner. Sanders found the Civilian idol pre-merge, saved MacArthur with it against a five-vote majority at F8, then played a second idol at F5 to force Jen out via the Double Shield — one of the season's most surgical double-plays. She won F4 immunity and dictated the Gerry boot. She lost a 4-4 jury tie to Alejandro by the narrowest possible margin. The most dangerous first-time player in franchise history.

rankings.name: Millie
rankings.reasoning: S9's most complete non-winner. Millie converted a Beware package into the Red Idol, used it at F8 to nullify a five-vote strike on Priya, and held her alliances together through one of the season's messiest post-merges. She won F5 immunity and was the last woman standing going into the finale. Her game had everything a winning résumé needs — she just ran into Bowie.

rankings.title: The Glow-Up Queen
rankings.reasoning: Lindsay won S1 with pure social grace and timing, beating Alejandro 5-4 while taking only 1 vote all season. She returned to reach the S2 Finale (lost 1-5-1 to Duncan) and then suffered a brutal S7 first boot (6-2-2). With a 33.3% win rate and 6 lifetime jury votes, her résumé is built on likability and trust webs. Rivalries with Jasmine/Shawn in S7 and minimal advantage play left her exposed in fast starts.
strengths: Durable alliance webs that scale to the endgame
Threat calibration without needing challenge runs
weaknesses: Vulnerable to early consensus boots in new casts
Light advantage usage and low challenge reliance
Can be labeled expendable when tribes seek calm
rankings.name: Lindsay
Chase
rankings.reasoning: S9's quiet closer. Chase arrived as the vlogger no one took seriously and finished as a finalist who made the right call in nearly every vote. He idoled himself at the revote that toppled Hicks, won the Vote Stopper to silence Julia at F4, and won F5 immunity to punch his ticket to the finale.

rankings.name: Duncan
rankings.title: The Delinquent
rankings.reasoning: CHAMPION season on the résumé. WINNER of S2. Legit jury respect (5 finalist votes). 5-3 vote. (S1). Won Final Tribal 5-1-1. (S2). Across 3 season(s): avg placement 7.33.
weaknesses: S4: early boot
17 votes total

rankings.name: Emma
rankings.title: The Comeback Kid
rankings.reasoning: CHAMPION season on the résumé. WINNER of S3. Reliable challenge contributor (5 total wins). High-end strategist (best rank: #2). 4-3-1 tiebreaker victory. (S3). Lost via rocks in a deadlock. (S6). Across 3 season(s): avg placement 9.33.
weaknesses: S6: rock draw chaos
S2: early boot

rankings.title: The Saboteur
rankings.reasoning: Scott is a perennial endgamer: S3 runner-up after a challenge-heavy run and S4 third with a record-setting idol hold and perfect F5 play. In S7 he rode an unbreakable duo with Shawn and even got covered by Kitty's idol before a consensus at 8th took him down. Across 3 seasons he tallied 7 challenge wins, 1 idol, and 4 jury votes. His power is obvious-and that's both his weapon and his curse.
strengths: Elite challenge output to steer tempo
Patience with idols, timing them for maximum swing
Ride-or-die partnerships that serve as shields
weaknesses: High visibility invites broad consensus votes
Duo tunnel-vision narrows endgame options
Jury warmth fluctuates after ruthless moves
rankings.name: Scott
Scarlett
rankings.title: The Puppetmaster
rankings.reasoning: Across 1 season(s): avg placement 4.00.
weaknesses: Lost fire-making
Only one season

rankings.title: The Hopeless Romantic
rankings.reasoning: Carrie was the S7 hub: she built Lawa #3, balanced Cody/MacArthur, and used a Vote Steal at F5 to engineer Tom's exit en route to FTC (1 vote). She managed early heat as a decoy, navigated Zoey and Pete feuds, and won 3 challenges with just 6 votes against. Her game emphasized shields, connectors, and timing over flash. The jury respected her control but favored MacArthur's power-social blend.
strengths: Endgame leverage with precise advantage usage
Threat assessment that sets up safe, repeatable votes
weaknesses: Perceived as manipulative by some opponents
Feud management (Zoey, Pete) can polarize jurors
Lower challenge intimidation than top-tier beasts
rankings.name: Carrie

rankings.title: The Indie Girl
rankings.reasoning: Zoey nearly perfected social play in S4, losing the closest finale 6-7 after building multiple 'unbreakable' bonds and nailing a massive idol save. In S3 she connected her way to 4th before Scott's betrayal, and in S7 a feud with Carrie led to a 5-2 pre-merge exit. With 8 challenge wins, 1 idol, and 6 jury votes, her ceiling is championship-level. Feud entanglements and late-game trust breaks have been her undoing.
strengths: Spot-on protection timing (idol saves, midgame flips)
weaknesses: Vulnerable to betrayal by close allies
Feuds (Carrie) create tunnel-vision targets
Can be targeted as a 'perfect finalist' archetype
rankings.name: Zoey
Jay
rankings.title: The Loyal Heart
rankings.reasoning: Navigated the merge rock draw. (S6). Finalist - 0 jury votes. (S6). Across 1 season(s): avg placement 3.00.
weaknesses: 0 jury votes (shutout)
No authorship
Passenger

rankings.name: Damien
rankings.reasoning: S9's endurance anchor. Damien played calm and precise all season — engineering Ripper as the merge shield target, winning F9 horror immunity, finding the Campers Hidden Idol at F7, and reaching F6 with a late Power Trio. He played his idol in a F5 double-idol standoff and only lost when Bowie beat him at fire.

rankings.title: The Athlete
rankings.reasoning: Sky's S5 was a challenge-forward run to a tied FTC loss, and in S7 she co-piloted with MacArthur before a late split and a 6th-place cut. She owns 4 total challenge wins and 2 idols across her career, with only 6 votes against. Her Mauhu backbone role helped shape merge plans like targeting Pete and Kitty. The late fracture with MacArthur and muted jury sway kept her off the podium in S7.
strengths: Stable alliance play with power players (MacArthur, Tom)
Composed midgame targeting and vote splits
weaknesses: Endgame fractures with close allies
Can be overshadowed by louder résumés
Limited control of final jury narratives
rankings.name: Sky
Ryan
rankings.title: The Gentle Giant
rankings.reasoning: Found and misplayed Galang idol. (S6). Won Pesky Palate immunity. (S6). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 5.50.
weaknesses: Wasted idol
Emma hatred war
11 votes S6

rankings.name: Kelly
rankings.title: The Protective Mom
rankings.reasoning: Lost Final 4 fire-making. (S6). Found and correctly played Tadhana idol at swap. (S6). Across 1 season(s): avg placement 4.00.
weaknesses: Lost fire-making
Multiple meltdowns

rankings.name: Trent
rankings.title: The Guitar Guy
rankings.reasoning: Reliable challenge contributor (4 total wins). High-end strategist (best rank: #3). 5-2 vote. (S2). Extreme hatred with Heather. (S1). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 6.50.
weaknesses: Never reached endgame

rankings.name: Gerry
rankings.reasoning: S8's most volatile power broker. Gerry survived multiple pre-merge ties through sheer force of personality, formed an Unbreakable Bond with Jen, and found the merged idol at F9. His masterpiece was psychological — episode by episode he dismantled Heather until she quit mid-game with the Super Idol unused in her pocket. He played his idol at F5 and fell at F4, one round short of the finale.

rankings.title: The Geek
rankings.reasoning: Cody's S7 was a connector masterclass: he stabilized under fire with Carrie, helped form Lawa #3, and timed the Legacy at F6 and an idol at F5 to reach FTC (2 votes). His S1 showed early social chops before a razor-thin 6-5 exit. Across 2 seasons he tallied 4 challenge wins and 1 idol found with only 9 votes against, but lost to MacArthur's résumé. Rivalries with Laurie and Pete tested him, yet his bonds with Carrie and MacArthur kept him alive.
strengths: Advantage timing under endgame pressure (Legacy + idol)
Reads that convert scattered votes into a stable majority
weaknesses: Jury comparison problem versus dominant allies
Can attract early-game heat before relationships stick
Public feuds (e.g., Laurie) bleed social capital
rankings.name: Cody

rankings.title: The Spy
rankings.reasoning: Tom played low-visibility until it mattered: he found the merge idol and fired it at F7 to cancel five votes and bounce Taylor, then was boxed out at F5 by a vote-steal plus double-idol wall. With 0 challenge wins, 1 idol found, and 13 votes against, his résumé hinged on timing, not volume. His bridges with Sky and MacArthur bought space even when he was out of step. The jury-facing story just wasn't big enough by F5.
strengths: Patience and precision with idol timing
Social links that reduce early and midgame exposure
weaknesses: Reactive gameplay limits jury résumé breadth
Becomes an easy consensus when shields are gone
Few marquee moves beyond the F7 idol shot
rankings.name: Tom
Jen
rankings.reasoning: S8's social monarch. Jen led the Civilians, built the widest coalition of any player in her season, won merge immunity, and helped blindside MacArthur on a revote at F6. She fell at F5 as the first victim of the Double Shield — Sanders and Alejandro played idols simultaneously to force a revote that ended her game. A dominant résumé cut one round short.

rankings.name: Geoff
rankings.title: The Party Surfer
rankings.reasoning: 7-2 vote. (S1). Won EoE return Ep 13. (S4). Across 3 season(s): avg placement 10.33.
weaknesses: Re-eliminated same episode
Vibes don't beat strategy

rankings.name: Bridgette
rankings.title: The Surfer Sweetheart
rankings.reasoning: Reliable challenge contributor (5 total wins). First boot - 4-3 vote. (S2). Used Safety Without Power (Ep 22). (S1). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 8.50.
weaknesses: Takes too many votes / limited jury support.

rankings.name: Ezekiel
rankings.title: The Farm Kid
rankings.reasoning: Advantage king/queen (3 idols/advantages found). Lost fire-making to Alejandro at F4. (S1). Legendary idol play on Alejandro at merge. (S2). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 5.00.
weaknesses: Never reached F3

rankings.name: Blaineley
rankings.title: The Media Maven
rankings.reasoning: Rock-draw survival at merge. (S6). Final 5 consensus boot. (S6). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 7.00.
weaknesses: Voted out by Kelly
Chaos volatility

rankings.name: Emmah
rankings.reasoning: S9's most fearless player. Emmah used Knowledge is Power to steal Wayne's Extra Vote and double-voted Ripper out in one of the season's boldest moves. She helped blindside Raj while he sat on an idol and won immunity at F7. She got caught in the F5 double-idol crossfire — the only vote she couldn't escape all season.

rankings.name: Gwen
rankings.title: The Goth Icon
rankings.reasoning: Elite challenge engine (9 total wins). Constant heat all game (26 votes against). 4 CONSECUTIVE immunities (Ep 11-14) - FRANCHISE RECORD!. (S4). Lost fire-making to Alejandro. (S4). Across 3 season(s): avg placement 9.33.
weaknesses: Lost fire-making F4
26 votes total

rankings.title: The Spoiled Princess
rankings.reasoning: Taylor ran S7 social lanes with Jacques and Carrie, flipped to form Lawa #3, and survived repeated shots from Scott/Shawn. She won two immunities and still got idol'd out at F7 despite the majority. With 3 challenge wins and 11 votes against, her threat level was obvious. Rivalries with Tom and the enforcer duo kept her in the crosshairs all merge.
strengths: Shield management while keeping personal threat poised
weaknesses: High visibility as a social hub draws idols and counters
Persistent rivalries limit endgame options
Reliance on tight duos invites targeted strikes
rankings.name: Taylor
Mike
rankings.title: The Many Faces
rankings.reasoning: Eliminated 2-0 (Sierra's perfect idol). (S4). Blindsided while Zoey held immunity. (S3). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 9.00.
weaknesses: Voted out by Zoey S4

rankings.name: Beth
rankings.title: The Sweetheart
rankings.reasoning: Constant heat all game (26 votes against). Opening rocks send to RI. (S6). Sent to RI on opening rocks; eliminated Episode 3 duel. (S6). Across 3 season(s): avg placement 9.33.
weaknesses: Little evidence of consistent strategic control.
Takes too many votes / limited jury support.

rankings.name: Dwayne
rankings.title: The Dad
rankings.reasoning: Early stability bonds with Geoff/Mickey. (S6). First juror. (S3). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 10.50.
weaknesses: First juror
Early merge boot

rankings.title: The Zombie Guy
rankings.reasoning: Shawn plays intense: S5's Beware Idol/Shot in the Dark gamble backfired, and S7 centered on a feared duo with Scott and a loud feud with Kitty. He exited 9th both times, including a 5-4 knife-edge in S7. With 2 challenge wins, 1 idol found, and 17 votes against, he's a force in motion more than a long-term manager. His rivalry heat made him a message boot once the board stabilized.
strengths: Drives targets and momentum alongside a loyal partner
Physical asset in team and individual phases
Unshaken under pressure in contentious votes
weaknesses: Paranoid gambles that cost votes and leverage
Public feuds (Kitty) that dominate agendas
Struggles to re-stabilize after shields leave
rankings.name: Shawn
Amy
rankings.title: The Mean Twin
rankings.reasoning: Across 1 season(s): avg placement 7.00.
weaknesses: Sisters rivalry

rankings.name: Julia
rankings.reasoning: S9's ice-cold tactician. Julia manipulated Raj into leaving with an idol in his pocket and joined the Misfits to take down Ripper. She was a genuine endgame threat until Bowie ripped her idol at Tribal with Knowledge is Power. She still helped pull the Priya blindside — then went out at F4 when Bowie and Chase combined their extras to silence her entirely.

rankings.name: Sugar
rankings.title: The Pageant Menace
rankings.reasoning: Across 1 season(s): avg placement 5.00.
weaknesses: Dave feud
Most targeted

rankings.name: Jo
rankings.title: The Alpha Athlete
rankings.reasoning: 5-3 revote at F9. (S4). Voted Emma at FTC. (S3). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 8.50.
weaknesses: Early merge boot

rankings.name: Priya
rankings.reasoning: S9's golden core. Priya built the New Red majority through cross-tribe bridging and was the strategic heartbeat of the post-merge. She survived a five-vote strike at F8 only because Millie played an idol on her and she burned an Extra Vote simultaneously. She fell in a coordinated 4-2-1 blindside at F6 that Bowie built specifically around removing her.

rankings.name: MK
rankings.reasoning: S9's stealthiest player. MK stayed low, kept her name off ballots, and won the F8 horror immunity when she needed it most. She never had a bad vote until Bowie's bloc went against her at F7 in a 4-2. A composed, analytical game that went further than it looked from the outside.

rankings.name: Dave
rankings.title: The Neurotic Strategist
rankings.reasoning: Across 1 season(s): avg placement 6.00.
weaknesses: Sugar feud
Idol victim

rankings.name: Heather
rankings.title: The Queen Bee
rankings.reasoning: Constant heat all game (27 votes against). Strong advantage game (2 idols/advantages found). 7-2 vote. (S2). 3-2 at Episode 5. (S4). Across 3 season(s): avg placement 10.67.
weaknesses: Takes too many votes / limited jury support.

rankings.name: Dawn
rankings.title: The Mystic
rankings.reasoning: Voted Scott at FTC. (S3). Consensus target Episode 9. (S3). Across 1 season(s): avg placement 8.00.
strengths: Strong average placement across seasons (deep runs).
weaknesses: Takes too many votes / limited jury support.
Little evidence of consistent strategic control.
Limited challenge contribution compared to the field.

rankings.name: Owen
rankings.title: The Big Buddy
rankings.reasoning: Third boot after idol play. (S2). 5-1* vote. (S2). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 9.50.
weaknesses: Hasn't converted strong runs into season wins.

rankings.name: Courtney
rankings.title: The Type-A Captain
rankings.reasoning: Constant heat all game (32 votes against). 3-2 vote. (S2). Major meltdown. (S4). Across 3 season(s): avg placement 11.33.
weaknesses: Takes too many votes / limited jury support.
Limited challenge contribution compared to the field.

rankings.title: The Sunshine
rankings.reasoning: Kitty thrives in chaos: 3 idols found across seasons and the S7 double-idol masterstroke that erased eight votes and boomeranged Pete out. In S6 she burned protection trying to shape power and got split-voted out; in S7 her feud with Shawn made every round volatile before she was cut at 11th. With 2 challenge wins and 28 votes against, she weaponizes fear and timing more than safety. She can be a loyal spark, but the blowback is immediate once the smoke clears.
strengths: Top-tier idol hunting and high-pressure reads
Creates chaos to reset vote maps in her favor
Protects allies at the exact moment stakes spike
weaknesses: Feuds (Shawn) escalate and narrow options
Idol fireworks invite instant follow-up boots
Social blowback after big plays limits longevity
rankings.name: Kitty

rankings.title: The Nature Girl
rankings.reasoning: Laurie lived in conflict and chaos in S7, feuding with Cody and drifting as a minority vote before serving as a merge number. She was finally cut 5-3-2 at 10th when the tribe wanted the heat to cool. With 3 challenge wins and 8 votes against, her presence was loud but rarely central. Ties to Taylor helped, but the Cody war defined her arc.
strengths: Fearless confrontation that can force clarity
Utility as a swing number in messy rounds
Loyalty to specific allies even from the bottom
weaknesses: Volatile social game alienates potential allies
Becomes a distraction voters tire of carrying
Thin jury equity after noisy midgame
rankings.name: Laurie
Max
rankings.title: The Evil Genius
rankings.reasoning: Across 1 season(s): avg placement 10.00.
strengths: Strong average placement across seasons (deep runs).
weaknesses: Limited challenge contribution compared to the field.

rankings.name: Leshawna
rankings.title: The Real One
rankings.reasoning: 6-1 vote. (S2). Second boot. (S2). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 12.00.
weaknesses: Takes too many votes / limited jury support.
Inconsistent results / early exits drag down average placement.

rankings.name: Axel
rankings.reasoning: S9's physical force. Axel survived the Ep2 rock draw, helped execute a clean 3-0 on Nichelle, and won merge immunity in Ep8. She was tight with Bowie and Ripper but found herself on the wrong side of the numbers post-merge and fell 4-1 at F9. Left with a stronger résumé than her placement shows.

rankings.title: The Old-School Coach
rankings.reasoning: Pete played the S7 middle with an Exile extra vote, clashed with Carrie/Kitty, and took a swing at Kitty on Idol Night-only to be the fallback when her double-idol detonated. He left at 12th despite 1 challenge win and 5 votes against. His reads were bold; the board just broke perfectly against him. Links with Taylor and Tom couldn't overcome the post-firewall pivot.
strengths: Grabs and uses advantages to press edges
Aggressive targeting of perceived power players
weaknesses: Miscalibrated timing versus idol stockpiles
Feuds painted him as an acceptable casualty
Insufficient shields once plans went public
rankings.name: Pete
Topher
rankings.title: The Host Wannabe
rankings.reasoning: Across 1 season(s): avg placement 11.00.
weaknesses: Limited challenge contribution compared to the field.

rankings.name: Wayne
rankings.reasoning: S9's loyal bruiser who drew the wrong cards at the wrong time. He won the Ep2 summit and picked up an Extra Vote — then lost it when Emmah lifted it with Knowledge is Power. He missed his Shot in the Dark at F9 and went out 5-0 the same episode Millie's idol and Priya's Extra Vote rewrote the ballot.

rankings.name: Josee
rankings.title: The Ice Queen
rankings.reasoning: Booted 6-1 as a consensus. (S6). Multiple meltdowns on Galang. (S6). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 12.50.
strengths: Low target profile and/or strong jury appeal.
weaknesses: Little evidence of consistent strategic control.
Limited challenge contribution compared to the field.
Inconsistent results / early exits drag down average placement.

rankings.name: Noah
rankings.title: The Snark King
rankings.reasoning: TRIPLE IDOL TRIBAL (played idol). (S4). 4-3 vote. (S1). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 12.00.
weaknesses: Takes too many votes / limited jury support.
Limited challenge contribution compared to the field.
Inconsistent results / early exits drag down average placement.

rankings.title: The Hunky Helper
rankings.reasoning: Brody's game leaned on grit: in S6 he outlasted heat, returned from Redemption, and even snagged an immunity before getting cut 4-2 at 7th. In S7 he never found structure and became the easy consensus boot at 18th. With 1 challenge win, 33 votes against, and zero idols, his threat came from endurance more than position. Loyal to Geoff and the Surfers vibe, he struggled to shake rival heat from Ryan and Mickey and later MacArthur.
strengths: Loyal duo play with Geoff to secure short-term safety
Can absorb being a decoy target without panicking
weaknesses: Limited numbers and weak majority pathways
Target magnet post-merge once redemption narrative formed
No advantage game to counter coordinated strikes
rankings.name: Brody
DJ
rankings.title: The Gentle Giant
rankings.reasoning: 9 votes in Episode 3. (S1). 4-1 vote at Final Five. (S2). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 13.50.
weaknesses: Takes too many votes / limited jury support.
Inconsistent results / early exits drag down average placement.
Limited challenge contribution compared to the field.

rankings.name: Harold
rankings.title: The Ninja Nerd
rankings.reasoning: Held idol without playing it. (S1). 4-3-2 vote. (S1). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 12.50.
weaknesses: Inconsistent results / early exits drag down average placement.
Takes too many votes / limited jury support.

rankings.name: Ripper
rankings.reasoning: S9's chaos engine. Ripper idoled four votes in Ep2 and caused the season's rock draw — one of the franchise's most dramatic early-game moments. He survived Ep3 when both he and Nichelle burned Shot in the Dark simultaneously, then coasted under Bowie's wing until Emmah double-voted him out at the merge.

rankings.title: The BFF
rankings.reasoning: 5-3-3*-1 vote. (S1). Part of MAPLV. (S1). Across 1 season(s): avg placement 12.00.
weaknesses: Limited challenge contribution compared to the field.
Inconsistent results / early exits drag down average placement.

rankings.title: The BFF
rankings.reasoning: PERFECT GAME: ZERO votes against. Rock draw elimination. (S1). Part of MAPLV. (S1). Across 1 season(s): avg placement 13.00.
strengths: Low target profile and/or strong jury appeal.
weaknesses: Inconsistent results / early exits drag down average placement.
Limited challenge contribution compared to the field.

rankings.reasoning: Across 1 season(s): avg placement 12.00.
weaknesses: Takes too many votes / limited jury support.

rankings.name: Sierra
rankings.title: The Superfan
rankings.reasoning: BIGGEST target profile in the cast (39 votes against). Perfect idol play (10 votes cancelled!). (S4). 6-4 vote. (S1). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 10.50.
strengths: Strong average placement across seasons (deep runs).
weaknesses: Takes too many votes / limited jury support.
Little evidence of consistent strategic control.
Limited challenge contribution compared to the field.

rankings.name: Anne Maria
rankings.title: The Jersey Diva
rankings.reasoning: WASTED idol on Zoey (0 votes!). (S4). Voted Emma at FTC. (S3). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 10.50.
weaknesses: Takes too many votes / limited jury support.
Little evidence of consistent strategic control.

rankings.name: Lightning
rankings.title: The Jock
rankings.reasoning: Constant heat all game (25 votes against). Strong advantage game (2 idols/advantages found). TRIPLE IDOL TRIBAL (Ep 5 Part 2). (S4). WORST idol play ever (wasted on Emma). (S3). Across 2 season(s): avg placement 12.00.
strengths: High win conversion rate when they play.
weaknesses: Takes too many votes / limited jury support.
Little evidence of consistent strategic control.
Limited challenge contribution compared to the field.

rankings.name: Stephanie
rankings.title: The Control Freak
rankings.reasoning: ZERO votes against across appearances. Voted out Episode 6 (6-1); threw an idol clue into the fire on RI. (S6). Burned an idol clue on RI. (S6). Across 1 season(s): avg placement 13.00.
strengths: Low target profile and/or strong jury appeal.
weaknesses: Little evidence of consistent strategic control.
Inconsistent results / early exits drag down average placement.
Limited challenge contribution compared to the field.

rankings.name: Brightly
rankings.reasoning: S9's most underrated player. Brightly orchestrated the Zee boot on Day 1 and moved immediately to target Emmah as a liability — Hicks flipped and saved Emmah instead, ending Brightly's game at F13. She had the clearest read on the season's social dynamics of any pre-merger but left too early for the résumé to reflect it.

rankings.title: The Jungle Queen
rankings.reasoning: Jasmine played steady and respected, but twice got clipped as the easy compromise: a mid-early exit in S5 and the first juror in S7 (7-4-2). She entered S7 merge looking like a stable finalist type, which made her the safest 'unity' boot. With 0 challenge wins, no idols, and 11 votes against, her capital was social, not flashy. Bonds with Cody/Brody helped, but a simmering dislike with Lindsay and split boards made her expendable.
strengths: Calm campsite leadership that lowers tribe friction
Respected presence that earns middle-position trust
weaknesses: Early-merge 'safe boot' aura in fractured votes
No advantages or immunity runs to deter consensus
Limited late-game leverage against strong blocs
rankings.name: Jasmine
Raj
rankings.reasoning: S9's most painful exit. Raj secured the Blue Beware advantage in Ep2 and completed his idol — then walked out of Ep7 tribal with it untouched after Julia manipulated the vote around him. He never saw it coming. An unfortunate end to a player with the social tools to go much deeper.

rankings.title: The Loyal Boyfriend
rankings.reasoning: Devin's S7 started with real social traction among the Fans (Carrie, Taylor), but swap attitudes from Favorites hardened and he fell 4-3. With 0 challenge wins, no idols, and 4 votes against, he lacked the shield or advantage to survive Scott/Shawn's push and Jacques' flip. His instincts to connect were sound; the cover wasn't. Once the board framed him as expendable, the window shut fast.
strengths: Quick to build early trust and soft alliances
Comfort playing the middle to keep options open
Solid social reads on Fan dynamics
weaknesses: No protection when cross-tribe heat rises
Couldn't lock a stable majority post-swap
Viewed as expendable by Favorites' core
rankings.name: Devin
Hicks
rankings.reasoning: S9's sharp early game cut short by bad timing. Hicks led the Day 1 consensus on Zee without taking any heat, then saved Emmah in a risky Ep4 pivot that blindsided Caleb. He fell at F14 in a revote triggered by Chase's idol — exactly the kind of collateral damage that ends good games fast.

rankings.title: The Hothead
rankings.reasoning: Lorenzo survived the S7 swap by flipping to help boot Brody, but the disciplined Mauhu trio (MacArthur/Sky/Tom) snuffed him 3-2 next. With 0 challenge wins, no idols, and 4 votes against, his opportunism bought only a brief extension. Bonding with Pete was real, but not enough to break structure. Once the majority solidified, he had no wedge.
strengths: Willingness to flip early for survival
Decent read on immediate majority safety
Useful pair dynamics (with Pete) to negotiate
weaknesses: Struggles to convert flips into long-term security
No advantage or immunity cover post-swap
Outside disciplined cores without a counter-plan

rankings.title: The Sleepy One
rankings.reasoning: Spud's S7 ended in joint-tribal crossfire where overlapping groups converged and he became the cleanest consensus (5-3-3). Scary Girl
rankings.reasoning: S9's wildcard gone too soon. Scary Girl pulled the short rock at the Ep2 0-0 idol council — the season's most chaotic early moment — and was gone before she had a chance to show what her game was. Impossible to evaluate fairly on placement alone.

rankings.name: Rock
rankings.reasoning: S8's unlucky early exit. Rock got caught in Civilian feud crossfire and was voted out 3-2 in Ep2 — a victim of tribal politics more than anything he did wrong. Left before his game had a chance to open up.

rankings.name: Zee
rankings.reasoning: S9's first boot. Zee came in with the right attitude and the wrong tempo — Brightly and Hicks read him as a liability on Day 1 and the tribe agreed 5-1. The most amicable departure of the season.
